population: 430 hospital-acquired influenza cases among 4110 hospitalized influenza-positive adults
study_design: Prospective multi-season observational study with subtype analysis
hospital_acquired_definition: Medical-record review used to confirm nosocomial origin
risk_factor_definition: Influenza subtype as a predictor of 30-day mortality among hospital-acquired influenza cases
finding_text: In multivariable analysis, influenza subtype was not predictive of 30-day mortality among hospital-acquired influenza cases; overall 30-day mortality was 9.3%
p_value: P=0.70 for H3N2 vs H1N1; P=0.58 for B vs H1N1
key_limitations: Comorbidities were not included in the mortality model
study_reported_implication: Subtype influenced dissemination more than downstream mortality
codeblue_translation: Do not overinterpret subtype as a mortality predictor once HAI is established
